steps.title: Find the mechanism explaining the timing effect
steps.content: The researchers conclude that early snow (before soil freezes) increases net CO₂. To support this, we need evidence of why early snow would favor respiration over plant uptake.

steps.title: Apply this mechanism to the choices
steps.content:
Choice D directly explains it: snow insulation allows soil respiration to continue during the period when plants aren't growing, creating a window of uncompensated CO₂ release—the mechanism driving net CO₂ increase.

The other three either isolate effects that don't account for the timing (A), suggest both processes are reduced without showing net CO₂ increase (B), or describe a geographic pattern unrelated to the temporal causation (C).

That matches choice D — D is correct.
